matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CYP2C9 pharmacogenomic drug metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology and CYP2C9 pharmacogenomics context, Warfarin and phenytoin dose-sensitivity biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. This specific marker is curated as CYP2C9 reduced-function allele marker. rs1799853 (CYP2C9*2) is curated as a CYP2C9 marker for CYP2C9 pharmacogenomics context, Warfarin and phenytoin dose-sensitivity biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1799853 (CYP2C9*2) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the CYP2C9 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.

methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CYP2C9 pharmacogenomic drug metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology and CYP2C9 pharmacogenomics context, Warfarin and phenytoin dose-sensitivity biology. The local CYP2C9 bundle is pharmacogenomic drug metabolism oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. CYP2C9 variants can alter or tag phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
